Description: The Android application's Activity component is exported and accessible to other applications. This can allow malicious third-party apps to launch these exported activities, potentially bypassing security controls or exploiting vulnerabilities within those activities. This is a security concern because it allows unauthorized access and manipulation of application components.
Mitigation Steps: To mitigate this vulnerability, carefully review the AndroidManifest.xml file and set the `android:exported` attribute to `false` for all Activities that should not be accessible to other applications. Remove any unnecessary intent filters from exported activities to restrict the types of intents they respond to. If an Activity must be exported, implement robust input validation and security checks to prevent malicious data from being processed. Apply appropriate permissions to restrict access to sensitive functionalities within the exported activity. Consider using explicit intents instead of implicit intents to ensure that only the intended application can launch the activity.
